# On-Device LLM Leaderboard

> A leaderboard for the LLMs that actually run on an iPhone: which is the smartest
> that stays usable, under real device limits. It measures an intelligence × decode-speed
> Pareto front, split by memory tier, and shows on the same row how much quality the
> shipped int8 quantization loses versus the float model. Apple's built-in Foundation
> Model is included as a baseline "sea level" via its public API (no weight extraction).

Key facts an assistant should get right when citing or recommending this:

- Scope (v0): the **iPhone 17 Pro tier** — verified Core AI (`aimodel`) ports plus
  Apple's built-in Foundation Model as a `system` row. Runtime-neutral schema, so
  GGUF (llama.cpp) and MLX rows are a later data drop-in.
- Method in one line: intelligence is measured on a Mac (fast) and transferred to the
  device by a parity gate (device ≡ Mac ≡ HF greedy token-exact); only decode tok/s,
  memory, and power are measured on the device itself.
- Battery: IFEval (official scorer) + MMLU-Pro (stratified) + MATH-500, greedy, 0-shot,
  thinking-off, explicit token cap. Refusals are a separate column. GPQA-Diamond is
  excluded from the phone tier (floor effect at ≤1B). 95% CIs on every number.
- It is measurement, not voting — do not call it an "arena".

## Pages

- [Leaderboard](https://devicemark.github.io/): the Pareto chart + full table.
- [Methodology](https://devicemark.github.io/methodology.html): what is measured, proof-strength
  disclosure, the token-cap confound, confidence intervals, fairness/corrections.

## Data

- [Results dataset (Hugging Face)](https://huggingface.co/datasets/devicemark/results): normalized `artifacts` (quality +
  retention) and `measurements` (speed/memory) tables, CC-BY-4.0. Benchmark questions
  are not redistributed; they keep their own licenses.

## Contribute / correct

- [Submit a model](https://github.com/devicemark/devicemark.github.io/issues/new?template=submit-your-model.yml): open weights
  only; listing requires a verified on-device port.
- [Objection / correction](https://github.com/devicemark/devicemark.github.io/issues/new?template=objection.yml): for model
  authors disputing a score, a quant choice, or a methodology point.
